\textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
  For composable open immersions \(h : Z \hookrightarrow Y\) and \(f : Y \hookrightarrow X\), restriction
  of sheaves of modules along the composite is canonically isomorphic to the composition of the two
  restrictions:
  \[
    \mathtt{restrictFunctor}\,(h \mathbin{;} f)
      \;\cong\; \mathtt{restrictFunctor}\,f \,\circ\, \mathtt{restrictFunctor}\,h .
  \]
  Its contravariant image under the dual --- \(\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\)
  (\cref{def:scheme_modules_dual_iso_of_iso}) of the forgetful image of
  \((\mathtt{restrictFunctorComp}\,h\,f).\mathrm{app}\,M\) --- is the dual-target pseudofunctoriality
  leg~4 of the cocycle \cref{lem:presheafdual_pullback_comparison_restrict}.
nodes.title: Restriction along a composite as a composition of restrictions \(\mathtt{restrictFunctorComp}\)

nodes.statement:
[Mate of a \(\mathtt{leftAdjointUniq}\) comparison is the identity]
  
  
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  Let \(F, F' : C_0 \to C_1\) be two left adjoints of one and the same right adjoint
  \(G : C_1 \to C_0\), via \(\mathrm{adj}_1 : F \dashv G\) and \(\mathrm{adj}_2 : F' \dashv G\). Then the
  mate (conjugate) of the comparison isomorphism \((\mathtt{leftAdjointUniq}\,\mathrm{adj}_1\,\mathrm{adj}_2).\mathrm{hom}\)
  under the conjugation bijection \(\mathtt{conjugateEquiv}\,\mathrm{adj}_2\,\mathrm{adj}_1\) is the identity
  natural transformation \(\mathbf{1}_G\). This is the abstract content behind every
  \(\mathtt{leftAdjointUniq}\) cocycle: the comparison transports the unit of one adjunction to the
  other and is therefore mate to \(\mathbf{1}_G\).

[Mate of the \(\mathtt{leftAdjointCompIso}\) hom is the reconciling inverse]
  
  
  
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  Given three adjunctions \(\mathrm{adj}_{01} : F_{01} \dashv G_{10}\),
  \(\mathrm{adj}_{12} : F_{12} \dashv G_{21}\), \(\mathrm{adj}_{02} : F_{02} \dashv G_{20}\) and a
  right-adjoint composition isomorphism \(e : G_{21} \circ G_{10} \cong G_{20}\), the mate (conjugate)
  of \((\mathtt{leftAdjointCompIso}\,\mathrm{adj}_{01}\,\mathrm{adj}_{12}\,\mathrm{adj}_{02}\,e).\mathrm{hom}\)
  under \(\mathtt{conjugateEquiv}\,\mathrm{adj}_{02}\,(\mathrm{adj}_{01}.\mathrm{comp}\,\mathrm{adj}_{12})\)
  equals \(e.\mathrm{inv}\). This is the companion of Mathlib's
  \(\mathtt{conjugateEquiv\_leftAdjointCompIso\_inv}\) (which computes the conjugate of the \(\mathrm{inv}\)).

\textit{Provided by Mathlib (\texttt{Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Adjunction/CompositionIso.lean}).}
  Given three adjunctions \(\mathrm{adj}_{01} : F_{01} \dashv G_{10}\),
  \(\mathrm{adj}_{12} : F_{12} \dashv G_{21}\), \(\mathrm{adj}_{02} : F_{02} \dashv G_{20}\) and a
  right-adjoint composition isomorphism \(e : G_{21} \circ G_{10} \cong G_{20}\), the hom of the
  left-adjoint composition isomorphism is \emph{fully explicit} at each object \(X\):
  \[
    (\mathtt{leftAdjointCompIso}\,\mathrm{adj}_{01}\,\mathrm{adj}_{12}\,\mathrm{adj}_{02}\,e).\mathrm{hom}.\mathrm{app}\,X
      \;=\; F_{12}.\mathrm{map}\bigl(F_{01}.\mathrm{map}\,(\mathrm{adj}_{02}.\mathrm{unit}.\mathrm{app}\,X)\bigr)
        \;\mathbin{;}\; F_{12}.\mathrm{map}\bigl(F_{01}.\mathrm{map}\,(e.\mathrm{inv}.\mathrm{app}\,\cdot)\bigr)
        \;\mathbin{;}\; F_{12}.\mathrm{map}\,(\mathrm{adj}_{01}.\mathrm{counit}.\mathrm{app}\,\cdot)
        \;\mathbin{;}\; \mathrm{adj}_{12}.\mathrm{counit}.\mathrm{app}\,\cdot,
  \]
  i.e. the unit of \(\mathrm{adj}_{02}\) pushed through \(F_{01}\) and \(F_{12}\), the reconciling
  \(e.\mathrm{inv}\), and the two counits of \(\mathrm{adj}_{01}\) and \(\mathrm{adj}_{12}\). When the
  reconciling iso is the identity (\(e = \mathtt{Iso.refl}\)) the middle \(e.\mathrm{inv}\) factor drops.
  This is the section-level component companion of the mate characterisation
  \cref{lem:conjugate_equiv_leftadjointcompiso_hom}.
nodes.title: Explicit component of the \(\mathtt{leftAdjointCompIso}\) hom

nodes.statement:
[Abstract \(H1\) cocycle: \(\mathtt{leftAdjointUniq}\) intertwines two \(\mathtt{leftAdjointCompIso}\)s]
  
  
  
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  Let \(F_\bullet\) and \(P_\bullet\) be two families of left adjoints sharing the right adjoints
  \(G_{\bullet\bullet}\) level by level: \(\mathrm{adjF}_{ij} : F_{ij} \dashv G_{ji}\) and
  \(\mathrm{adjP}_{ij} : P_{ij} \dashv G_{ji}\) for \(ij \in \{01, 12, 02\}\), together with a single
  shared right-adjoint composition isomorphism \(e : G_{21} \circ G_{10} \cong G_{20}\). Write
  \(H1_{ij} = \mathtt{leftAdjointUniq}\,\mathrm{adjF}_{ij}\,\mathrm{adjP}_{ij} : F_{ij} \cong P_{ij}\)
  for the comparison isomorphisms and \(FC, PC\) for the two
  \(\mathtt{leftAdjointCompIso}\)s built from the \emph{same} \(e\). Then the \(H1\)s intertwine the two
  composition isomorphisms:
  \[
    FC.\mathrm{hom} \;\mathbin{;}\; (H1_{02}).\mathrm{hom}
      \;=\; \bigl((H1_{01}).\mathrm{hom} \,\triangleright\, F_{12}\bigr)
        \;\mathbin{;}\; \bigl(P_{01} \,\triangleleft\, (H1_{12}).\mathrm{hom}\bigr)
        \;\mathbin{;}\; PC.\mathrm{hom} .
  \]
  This is the abstract \(H1\) cocycle instantiated in the Cone~B/c.2 lemma
  \cref{lem:presheafdual_h1_cocycle}, and the generic engine behind
  \(\mathtt{conjugateEquiv\_restrictFunctorComp\_inv}\)
  (\cref{lem:conjugate_equiv_restrictfunctorcomp_inv}): both reduce a composite comparison to a chain
  over the two composition isomorphisms.

\textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  Let \(h : Z \hookrightarrow Y\) and \(f : Y \hookrightarrow X\) be composable open immersions, write
  \(\varphi_g = g.\mathtt{toRingCatSheafHom}.\mathrm{hom}\), and let
  \[
    H1_g \;=\; (\mathtt{pushforwardPushforwardAdj}\,\cdots).\mathtt{leftAdjointUniq}\,
      (\mathtt{pullbackPushforwardAdjunction}\,\varphi_g)
      \;\colon\; \mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta_g \;\cong\; \mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi_g
  \]
  be the left-adjoint-uniqueness isomorphism re-presenting the presheaf pullback as the pushforward
  (Stage~H1 of \cref{def:presheafdual_pullback_comparison}). Then \(H1\) satisfies a \emph{cocycle}
  (composition) law over the factorisation \(h \mathbin{;} f\): on the pullback (source) flank it is
  threaded by the presheaf pseudofunctoriality iso \(\mathtt{pullbackComp}\,\varphi_f\,\varphi_h\)
  (\cref{lem:presheafofmodules_pullbackcomp}) and on the pushforward (target) flank by the
  restriction-composition iso \(\mathtt{restrictFunctorComp}\,h\,f\)
  (\cref{lem:scheme_modules_restrictfunctorcomp}, equivalently the pushforward composition
  \(\mathtt{pushforwardComp}\)). Writing \(FC = \mathtt{leftAdjointCompIso}\) for the left-adjoint
  composition isomorphism of the three pushforward adjunctions
  \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta_g \dashv \mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi_g\), reconciled by the presheaf
  pushforward composition \(\mathtt{pushforwardComp}\,\varphi_f\,\varphi_h\), the realized cocycle is
  the \emph{hom}-form identity
  \[
    FC.\mathrm{hom} \;\mathbin{;}\; (H1_{h \mathbin{;} f}).\mathrm{hom}
      \;=\; \bigl((H1_f).\mathrm{hom} \,\triangleright\, \mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta_h\bigr)
        \;\mathbin{;}\; \bigl(\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi_f \,\triangleleft\, (H1_h).\mathrm{hom}\bigr)
        \;\mathbin{;}\; (\mathtt{pullbackComp}\,\varphi_f\,\varphi_h).\mathrm{hom} ,
  \]
  stated as a faithful \(\forall\)-strengthening over the three pushforward adjunctions
  \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta_g \dashv \mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi_g\)
  (\(g \in \{f,\, h,\, h\mathbin{;}f\}\)); the consumer instantiates it with the matching
  \(\mathtt{pushforwardPushforwardAdj}\) adjunctions. Here \(\triangleright\) is right-whiskering
  (\(\alpha \triangleright F = \mathtt{whiskerRight}\,\alpha\,F\)) and \(\triangleleft\) left-whiskering
  (\(F \triangleleft \alpha = \mathtt{whiskerLeft}\,F\,\alpha\)). Inverting the identity (swap \(f\)
  and \(h\) in the two middle legs and replace \((\mathtt{pullbackComp}\,\varphi_f\,\varphi_h).\mathrm{hom}\)
  by its \(\mathrm{inv}\)) yields the form in which \(\theta\)'s \(H1\)-prefix is recombined inside
  \cref{lem:presheafdual_pullback_comparison_restrict}.
  This is the dual-flank analogue of \cref{lem:conjugate_equiv_pullbackcomp_inv}
  (\(\mathtt{conjugateEquiv\_pullbackComp\_inv}\)) and of
  \(\mathtt{conjugateEquiv\_restrictFunctorComp\_inv}\)
  (\cref{lem:conjugate_equiv_restrictfunctorcomp_inv}). This is the \(H1\)-flank of the cocycle
  \cref{lem:presheafdual_pullback_comparison_restrict}.
nodes.title: Cone B / c.2(a): cocycle law of the adjunction-uniqueness iso \(H1\) over the immersion factorisation

nodes.statement:
[Generic \(H1\)-cancellation skeleton for the c.2 cocycle]
  
  
  
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  Work in an arbitrary category \(C\) with objects \(A_1, \dots, A_9\) and the morphisms appearing in
  the c.2 reduction. Given the eight structural facts of that reduction --- the two iso cancellations
  \(\mathtt{aHinv} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{aH} = \mathbf{1}\) (of \(H1_{h \mathbin{;} f}\)) and
  \(\mathtt{fcinv} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{fc} = \mathbf{1}\) (of \(FC\)), the abstract \(H1\) cocycle
  \(\mathtt{fc} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{aH} = \mathtt{phf} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{hh} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{pc}\)
  (instantiating \cref{lem:presheafdual_h1_cocycle}), the \(\mathtt{pullbackComp}\) cancellation
  \(\mathtt{pc} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{p0} = \mathbf{1}\), the single \(H1_h.\mathrm{hom}\) naturality
  slide \(\mathtt{hh} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{Pfhif} = \mathtt{Pushhif} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{hhdmf}\), the
  \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta_h\)-functoriality fold
  \(\mathtt{phf} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{Pushhif} = \mathtt{pushSDTf}\), the \(H1_h\) round-trip
  \(\mathtt{hhdmf} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{Hhinv} = \mathbf{1}\), and the residual \((**)\)
  \(\mathtt{fc} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{s} = \mathtt{pushSDTf} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{sDTh} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{p3}\)
  --- the c.2 goal identity
  \(\mathtt{aHinv} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{s}
    = \mathtt{p0} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{Pfhif} \mathbin{;} (\mathtt{Hhinv} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{sDTh}) \mathbin{;} \mathtt{p3}\)
  holds. This is the instance-agnostic engine that performs the entire \(H1\)-cancellation of
  \cref{lem:presheafdual_pullback_comparison_restrict}; the consumer supplies its eight hypotheses at
  the concrete pushforward/pullback morphisms (the cocycle hypothesis being
  \cref{lem:presheafdual_h1_cocycle}, itself \cref{lem:leftadjointuniq_leftadjointcompiso_comm} at the
  three pushforward adjunctions, reconciled by \cref{lem:conjugate_equiv_leftadjointcompiso_hom}).

\textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  Let \(j : V \hookrightarrow U\) be the chart morphism, \(j \mathbin{;} \iota_U = \iota_V\), and
  \(M \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\). The presheaf dual base-change comparison \(\theta\)
  (\cref{def:presheafdual_pullback_comparison}) satisfies a \emph{cocycle} (composition) law over the
  immersion factorisation, in the \emph{exact dual shape} of the tensor composition coherence
  \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map_basechange}: the \(\iota_V\)-instance \(\theta_{\iota_V, M}\) factors
  through the \(j\)-pullback of the \(\iota_U\)-instance and the \(j\)-instance \emph{at the pushforward
  object} \((\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta_{\iota_U}).\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}}\) --- the
  base-change image of \(M_{\mathrm{val}}\), \emph{not} the restricted module \(M|_{\iota_U}\) ---
  conjugated by the presheaf pullback pseudofunctoriality iso \(\mathtt{pullbackComp}\,j\,\iota_U\) on the
  source and by its contravariant transport on the dual target:
  \[
    \theta_{\iota_V, M}
      = (\mathtt{pullbackComp}\,j\,\iota_U).\mathrm{inv}_{\,\mathtt{dual}\,M_{\mathrm{val}}}
        \;\mathbin{;}\; (\mathtt{pullback}\,j)\bigl(\theta_{\iota_U, M}\bigr)
        \;\mathbin{;}\; \theta_{j,\ M|_{\iota_U}}
        \;\mathbin{;}\; \mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\bigl(\mathtt{forget}.\mathrm{mapIso}\,
          ((\mathtt{restrictFunctorComp}\,j\,\iota_U).\mathrm{app}\,M)\bigr).\mathrm{hom}.
  \]
  Stating the middle factor at the restricted module \(M|_{\iota_U}\) is type-correct because the
  object-identification iso \(\mathtt{pushforwardObjValRestrictIso}\)
  (\cref{def:pushforward_obj_val_restrict_iso}, bridge c.1) is \(\mathtt{Iso.refl}\): the underlying
  presheaf \((M|_{\iota_U})_{\mathrm{val}}\) is \emph{definitionally} the pushforward object
  \((\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta_{\iota_U}).\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}}\) (the presheaf-level pushforward
  composition being the identity), so the codomain of \((\mathtt{pullback}\,j)(\theta_{\iota_U, M})\)
  --- namely
  \((\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi_j).\mathrm{obj}\bigl(\mathtt{dual}((\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi_{\iota_U}).\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}})\bigr)\)
  --- already matches the domain of \(\theta_{j,\ M|_{\iota_U}}\) with no intervening transport. This is
  the form consumed by \cref{lem:dual_restrict_iso_restrict_compat}. On the dual (target) flank the
  contravariant transport is realised by \(\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\)
  (\cref{def:scheme_modules_dual_iso_of_iso}) of the forgetful image of the restriction-composition iso
  \((\mathtt{restrictFunctorComp}\,j\,\iota_U).\mathrm{app}\,M\)
  (\cref{lem:scheme_modules_restrictfunctorcomp}); this pseudofunctoriality leg appears in place of the
  \(\varrho \otimes \varrho\) of the tensor side. This is the dual analogue of the composition role of
  \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map_natural} inside \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map_basechange}.
nodes.title: Bridge dual-(c): cocycle law of the presheaf dual base-change comparison \(\theta\) over \(\mathtt{pullbackComp}\)

nodes.statement:
[L1: folded composition cocycle for \(\mathtt{dual\_restrict\_iso}\) along a composite]
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference (dual mirror of the
  tensor-flank composition law \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso_restrict_compat}).}
  Let \(f : Z \hookrightarrow Y\) and \(g : Y \hookrightarrow X\) be composable open immersions
  (\emph{general} --- not tied to the chart \(j\)) and \(M \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\). Write
  \(D(h) := \mathtt{dual\_restrict\_iso}\,h : (\mathtt{dual}\,A)|_h \cong \mathtt{dual}(A|_h)\)
  (\cref{lem:dual_restrict_iso}). The dual-restriction comparison along the composite \(f \mathbin{;} g\)
  factors --- \emph{entirely at the folded Scheme-\(\mathtt{dual}\) level}, so that every junction is a
  Scheme-dual \(\leftrightarrow\) Scheme-dual identification and no \(\mathtt{sheafification}\)-colimit
  seam appears in the statement --- as
  \[
    D(f \mathbin{;} g)
      \;=\; \bigl(\mathtt{restrictFunctorComp}\,f\,g\bigr).\mathrm{app}\,(\mathtt{dual}\,M)
        \;\mathbin{;}\; (\mathtt{restrict}\,f)\,D(g)
        \;\mathbin{;}\; D(f)\,\bigl(M|_g\bigr)
        \;\mathbin{;}\; \mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,\bigl((\mathtt{restrictFunctorComp}\,f\,g).\mathrm{app}\,M\bigr),
  \]
  where \(\mathtt{restrictFunctorComp}\,f\,g\) (\cref{lem:scheme_modules_restrictfunctorcomp}) is the
  restriction-composition pseudofunctoriality iso \(A|_{f\mathbin{;}g} \cong (A|_g)|_f\) and
  \(\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\) (\cref{def:scheme_modules_dual_iso_of_iso}) is its contravariant dual image.
  Choosing the \(\mathtt{restrictFunctorComp}\)-induced head (rather than a \(\mathtt{pullbackComp}\)
  one) keeps both flanks in the restriction world, hence syntactically folded.

[L2: folded \(\mathtt{restrictFunctorCongr}\) coherence for \(\mathtt{dual\_restrict\_iso}\)]
  
  
  
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  Let \(f, g : Y \hookrightarrow X\) be open immersions with \(f = g\) (witnessed by \(hf\)), and
  \(M \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\). The dual-restriction comparison is coherent with the
  restriction congruence \(\mathtt{restrictFunctorCongr}\,hf : A|_f \cong A|_g\), at the folded
  Scheme-\(\mathtt{dual}\) level (both junctions Scheme-dual \(\leftrightarrow\) Scheme-dual, seam-free):
  \[
    \bigl(\mathtt{restrictFunctorCongr}\,hf\bigr).\mathrm{app}\,(\mathtt{dual}\,M)
        \;\mathbin{;}\; \mathtt{dual\_restrict\_iso}\,g\,M
      \;=\; \mathtt{dual\_restrict\_iso}\,f\,M
        \;\mathbin{;}\; \mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,\bigl((\mathtt{restrictFunctorCongr}\,hf).\mathrm{app}\,M\bigr)^{-1}.
  \]
  This is the dual mirror of \(\mathtt{pullbackTensorMap\_pullbackCongr}\), used to convert the
  \(\iota_V\)-leg of S3 to the composite \(j \mathbin{;} \iota_U\). \emph{(This brick is already verified
  in Lean: \(\mathtt{dual\_restrict\_iso\_pullbackCongr}\), private, axiom-clean.)}

[S3: dual-restriction comparison commutes with further restriction (clean core)]
  
  
  
  Let \(M \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) and let \(j : V \hookrightarrow U\) be the chart
  morphism above. Write \(D(f) := \mathtt{dual\_restrict\_iso}\,f :
  (\mathtt{dual}\,M)|_f \cong \mathtt{dual}\bigl(M|_f\bigr)\) (\cref{lem:dual_restrict_iso}). The
  \emph{bare} dual-restriction comparison is natural under further restriction along \(j\): modulo
  the reindexing \(\varrho = \mathtt{restrictCompReindex}\,j\) (\cref{def:restrictcompreindex}),
  with its contravariant image \(\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,\varrho\)
  (\cref{def:scheme_modules_dual_iso_of_iso}) on the dual flank,
  \[
    D(\iota_V)
      \;=\; \varrho_{\mathtt{dual}\,M}
        \mathbin{;} (\mathtt{restrict}\,j)\,D(\iota_U)
        \mathbin{;} \mathtt{dual\_restrict\_iso}\,j\,\bigl(M|_{\iota_U}\bigr)
        \mathbin{;} \mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,\varrho_M .
  \]
  This square proves \emph{only} the \(\mathtt{dual\_restrict\_iso}\) immersion-naturality; the
  trivialisation step's \((\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,e^M)^{-1}\) transport and the datum refinement
  \(e^M \mapsto \mathtt{restrictIsoUnitOfLE}\,h_{VU}\,e^M\) are \emph{not} part of the claim --- they
  telescope in the parent (\cref{lem:trivialisation_restrict_compat}).

\textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
  For any oplax monoidal functor \(F\), Mathlib supplies the \emph{unit comparator}
  \(\eta_F : F(\mathbf 1) \to \mathbf 1\), the oplax counterpart of the unit map. Applied to the
  oplax-monoidal presheaf pullback \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.pullback}\,\varphi\)
  (\cref{lem:presheaf_pullback_oplaxmonoidal}) along the chart immersion
  \(j : V \hookrightarrow U\), \(\varphi = j.\mathtt{toRingCatSheafHom}\), it furnishes the bare
  oplax unit comparison
  \[
    \eta(\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi) :
      (\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}\,\mathbf 1_U \longrightarrow \mathbf 1_V .
  \]
  This is the \emph{conceptual} pullback-flank unit map. Its invertibility is \emph{not} asserted
  here. The actual pullback-side comparator \(p\) used in the dual base-change theory is built
  instead as
  the H1 transport of the pushforward comparator \(q\); see
  \cref{def:presheaf_pullback_unit_iso}.
nodes.title: Oplax-monoidal unit map $\eta$ of the presheaf pullback --- conceptual anchor

nodes.statement:
[Pullback-side unit comparator $p$ --- the H1 realization]
  
  
  
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  Let \(j : V \hookrightarrow U\) be the chart immersion, \(\varphi = j.\mathtt{toRingCatSheafHom}\)
  the pullback ring map, and \(\beta\) the corresponding (sectionwise-bijective) pushforward ring
  map. The pullback-side unit comparator used throughout the dual base-change theory is the
  isomorphism
  \[
    p := \mathtt{presheafPullbackUnitIso}\,j :
      (\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}\,\mathbf 1_U \;\cong\; \mathbf 1_V ,
  \]
  realized \emph{not} as the bare oplax \(\eta\) (\cref{def:presheaf_pullback_unit_eta}) but through
  the \emph{H1 reconciliation}. Both \(\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi\) and the open-immersion
  pushforward \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta\) are left adjoints of the same right adjoint
  \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi\); the left-adjoint-uniqueness isomorphism
  \[
    H_1 := \mathtt{leftAdjointUniq}\bigl(\mathtt{pushforwardPushforwardAdj},\,
      \mathtt{pullbackPushforwardAdjunction}\,\varphi\bigr) :
      \mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\; \mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi
  \]
  identifies the two left adjoints (\cref{lem:unit_leftadjointuniq_hom_app},
  \cref{lem:pushforwardpushforwardadj_unit_app_app_apply}). Evaluated at the structure-sheaf unit
  \(\mathbf 1_U\), it carries the pullback flank to the pushforward flank, on which the pushforward
  comparator \(q = \mathtt{presheafPushforwardUnitIso}\,j\) (\cref{def:presheaf_pushforward_unit_iso})
  contracts to \(\mathbf 1_V\). Composing,
  \[
    p \;=\; (H_1.\mathrm{app}\,\mathbf 1_U)^{-1} \,\mathbin{;}\, q ,
  \]
  the H1-transport of \(q\). This is exactly the identification by which the two flank comparators
  are matched at the structure-sheaf unit in the \(\theta\)-at-unit bridge
  \cref{lem:presheafdualunitiso_pullback_natural}.

\textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
  For any lax monoidal functor \(G\), Mathlib supplies the \emph{unit comparator}
  \(\varepsilon_G : \mathbf 1 \to G(\mathbf 1)\). Applied to the lax-monoidal presheaf pushforward
  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.pushforward}\,\beta\) (\cref{lem:presheaf_pushforward_laxmonoidal}) along
  the chart immersion, it gives the bare lax unit comparison
  \[
    \varepsilon(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta) :
      \mathbf 1_V \longrightarrow (\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta).\mathrm{obj}\,\mathbf 1_U ,
  \]
  the presheaf analogue of \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit}\). This is the
  \emph{conceptual} pushforward-flank unit map; its invertibility is supplied not by the lax
  structure but by the strong monoidality of \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta\) for an open immersion
  (\cref{lem:presheaf_pushforward_beta_monoidal}), and the resulting comparator \(q\) is recorded in
  \cref{def:presheaf_pushforward_unit_iso}.
nodes.title: Lax-monoidal unit map $\varepsilon$ of the presheaf pushforward --- conceptual anchor

nodes.statement:
[Strong monoidality of the open-immersion presheaf pushforward $\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta$]
  
  
  
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  Let \(f : Y \to X\) be an open immersion and \(\beta = \mathtt{whiskerRight}\,\alpha\,
  (\mathtt{forget}_2)\) the induced structure-ring comparison, where
  \(\alpha.\mathrm{app}\,U = (f.\mathtt{appIso}\,U)^{-1}\). Since \(f\) is an open immersion each
  \(\alpha.\mathrm{app}\,U\) is invertible, hence \(\beta\) is sectionwise bijective. Consequently the
  presheaf pushforward factors as the strong-monoidal composite
  \[
    \mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta \;=\;
      \mathtt{pushforward}_0^{\mathrm{Comm}} \,\circ\, \mathtt{restrictScalars}\,\beta ,
  \]
  in which the topological pushforward \(\mathtt{pushforward}_0^{\mathrm{Comm}}\) is strong monoidal
  and \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\,\beta\) is strong monoidal because \(\beta\) is sectionwise
  bijective (\(\mathtt{restrictScalarsMonoidalOfBijective}\)). Hence \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta\)
  carries a \emph{strong}-monoidal structure, upgrading the ambient lax instance
  (\cref{lem:presheaf_pushforward_laxmonoidal}); in particular its unit comparator
  \(\varepsilon\) and tensorator \(\mu\) are isomorphisms.

[Pushforward-side unit comparator $q$ --- the strong-monoidal $\varepsilon$ inverse]
  
  
  
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  In the open-immersion setting above, the strong monoidality of \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta\)
  (\cref{lem:presheaf_pushforward_beta_monoidal}) makes the unit comparator an isomorphism
  \(\varepsilon_{\mathrm{iso}}(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta) :
    \mathbf 1_V \cong (\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta).\mathrm{obj}\,\mathbf 1_U\). The pushforward-side
  unit comparator is its inverse,
  \[
    q := \varepsilon_{\mathrm{iso}}(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta)^{-1} :
      (\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta).\mathrm{obj}\,\mathbf 1_U \;\cong\; \mathbf 1_V ,
  \]
  the strong-monoidal \(\varepsilon_{\mathrm{iso}}\) inverse (not the bare lax \(\varepsilon\) of
  \cref{def:presheaf_pushforward_unit_eps}).

[Carrier-value of the pushforward comparator: $q$ is the $f.\mathtt{appIso}$-conjugation on carriers]
  
  
  
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  This is the unit-side analogue of the tensorator collapse
  \cref{lem:pushforward_mu_appiso_collapse}. With \(f : Y \to X\) an open immersion,
  \(\beta\) the (sectionwise-bijective) structure-ring comparison, and
  \(q = \mathtt{presheafPushforwardUnitIso}\,f = \varepsilon_{\mathrm{iso}}(\mathtt{pushforward}\,
  \beta)^{-1}\) (\cref{def:presheaf_pushforward_unit_iso}), the sectionwise carrier action of
  \(q.\mathrm{hom} = \varepsilon_{\mathrm{iso}}(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta)^{-1}\) at an
  \emph{arbitrary} carrier element \(y\) is, over a section \(V\),
  \[
    \bigl(q.\mathrm{hom}\bigr)_V (y)
      \;=\; (f.\mathtt{appIso}\,V).\mathrm{hom}\,(y) ,
  \]
  the \(f.\mathtt{appIso}\)-conjugation of \(y\). The unit case \(y = 1\) is the motivating
  instance; the general form is the one the L1\('\) consumer
  \cref{lem:presheafdualunitiso_pullback_natural} requires (it instantiates at a non-unit
  carrier).

[Abstract $\varepsilon$-value of $\mathtt{restrictScalars}$ on carriers]
  
  
  
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  For ring-presheaves \(R, S : \mathcal C^{\mathrm{op}} \to \mathtt{CommRingCat}\) and a natural
  transformation \(\alpha : R \to S\), the lax unit comparator \(\varepsilon\) of
  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars}\,\alpha\) acts sectionwise on carriers by the ring map
  \(\alpha\): over a section \(W\), \(\varepsilon.\mathrm{app}\,W\,(r) = (\alpha.\mathrm{app}\,W).\mathrm{hom}\,(r)\).
  This is the abstract \(\{R,S : \mathcal C^{\mathrm{op}} \to \mathtt{CommRingCat}\}\) companion of the
  unit-value template \cref{lem:restrictscalars_oplaxmonoidal_eta_app_one}; the abstract framing makes
  the ambient \(\mathtt{CommRing}\) instance native (the concrete post-\(\mathtt{forget}_2\) Scheme
  carrier exposes only \(\mathtt{Ring}\)).

[Carrier-value of the pushforward comparator inverse $q.\mathrm{inv} = \varepsilon$]
  
  
  
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  In the open-immersion setting, with \(q = \mathtt{presheafPushforwardUnitIso}\,f\)
  (\cref{def:presheaf_pushforward_unit_iso}), the inverse \(q.\mathrm{inv} =
  \varepsilon(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta)\) acts sectionwise on carriers as the inverse structure-ring
  transport: over a section \(V\),
  \(\bigl(q.\mathrm{inv}\bigr)_V (w) = (f.\mathtt{appIso}\,V).\mathrm{inv}\,(w)\).
  This is the \(\varepsilon\)-side companion of \cref{lem:pushforward_beta_unit_eps_app_one}; it is
  consumed both inside that lemma and directly by the L1\('\) flank
  \cref{lem:presheafdualunitiso_pullback_natural}.

[S4a / L1$'$: $\theta$-at-unit bridge --- the presheaf dual base-change comparison at the structure sheaf is the dual-of-unit iso]
  
  
  
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  Let \(j : V \hookrightarrow U\) be the chart immersion with \(j \mathbin{;} \iota_U = \iota_V\),
  \(\varphi = j.\mathtt{toRingCatSheafHom}\) the pullback ring map, and \(\beta\) the corresponding
  pushforward ring map. Evaluate the presheaf dual base-change comparison
  \(\theta_M = \mathtt{presheafDualPullbackComparison}\,j\,M\)
  (\cref{def:presheafdual_pullback_comparison}) at the structure-sheaf unit
  \(M = \mathbf 1_U = \mathtt{SheafOfModules.unit}\,U.\mathtt{ringCatSheaf}\). Because \(\theta\) lands
  the dual on the pushforward flank, this gives
  \[
    \theta_{\mathbf 1} : \mathtt{pullback}_\varphi(\mathtt{dual}\,\mathbf 1_U)
      \;\cong\; \mathtt{dual}\bigl((\mathtt{pushforward}_\beta)\,\mathbf 1_U\bigr).
  \]
  Two \emph{distinct} unit comparators enter, one per flank:
  \begin{itemize}
    \item the pullback-side comparator
      \(p := \mathtt{presheafPullbackUnitIso}\,j : \mathtt{pullback}_\varphi\,\mathbf 1_U \cong \mathbf 1_V\)
      (\cref{def:presheaf_pullback_unit_iso}), the H1-transport \((H_1.\mathrm{app}\,\mathbf 1_U)^{-1}
      \mathbin{;} q\) of the pushforward comparator;
    \item the pushforward-side comparator
      \(q := \varepsilon_{\mathrm{iso}}(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta)^{-1} :
        (\mathtt{pushforward}_\beta)\,\mathbf 1_U \cong \mathbf 1_V\)
      (\cref{def:presheaf_pushforward_unit_iso}), the inverse of the strong-monoidal unit comparator
      of the pushforward.
  \end{itemize}
  Modulo the eval-at-\(1\) dual-of-unit isomorphism \(\mathtt{presheafDualUnitIso}\)
  (\cref{def:presheaf_dual_unit_iso}) on the \(U\)- and \(V\)-flanks, \(\theta_{\mathbf 1}\)
  \emph{is} the dual-of-unit identification, with the two comparators on opposite flanks: \(q\) is
  transported contravariantly through \(\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\) onto the pushforward codomain of
  \(\theta_{\mathbf 1}\), while \(p\) closes the pullback domain on the right:
  \[
    \theta_{\mathbf 1}
      \,\mathbin{;}\, \bigl(\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,q\bigr)^{-1}
      \,\mathbin{;}\, \mathtt{presheafDualUnitIso}_V
    \;=\; \mathtt{pullback}_\varphi\bigl(\mathtt{presheafDualUnitIso}_U\bigr)
      \,\mathbin{;}\, p,
  \]
  both sides being isomorphisms
  \(\mathtt{pullback}_\varphi(\mathtt{dual}\,\mathbf 1_U) \cong \mathbf 1_V\). This is the
  unit-specialisation of the immersion-naturality \cref{lem:presheafdual_pullback_restrict_natural}
  and the immersion analogue of the unit-automorphism core
  \cref{lem:presheafdualunitiso_naturality}.

[S4a / L2$'$: unit assembly --- the dual-restriction comparison at the unit intertwines the dual-unit contractions]
  
  
  
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  With \(j : V \hookrightarrow U\) as above, write \(\mathbf 1_U, \mathbf 1_V\) for the structure
  sheaves, \(u_j = \mathtt{unitRestrictIso}\,j\) (\cref{def:unitrestrictiso}) for the chart-scheme
  unit identification \(\mathbf 1_U|_j \cong \mathbf 1_V\), and
  \(\mathtt{dual\_unit\_iso}_U, \mathtt{dual\_unit\_iso}_V\) for the dual-unit contractions
  (\cref{lem:dual_unit_iso}). The \emph{bare} dual-restriction comparison
  \(\mathtt{dual\_restrict\_iso}\,j\,(\mathbf 1_U) : (\mathtt{dual}\,\mathbf 1_U)|_j \cong
  \mathtt{dual}(\mathbf 1_U|_j)\) (\cref{lem:dual_restrict_iso}) intertwines the two contractions
  with the unit-restriction identification:
  \[
    (\mathtt{restrict}\,j)\,\mathtt{dual\_unit\_iso}_U \,\mathbin{;}\, u_j
      \;=\; \mathtt{dual\_restrict\_iso}\,j\,(\mathbf 1_U)
        \,\mathbin{;}\, \bigl(\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,u_j\bigr)^{-1}
        \,\mathbin{;}\, \mathtt{dual\_unit\_iso}_V,
  \]
  both sides being isomorphisms \((\mathtt{dual}\,\mathbf 1_U)|_j \cong \mathbf 1_V\).

[RES1 (dual-flank reconciliation): the forgotten sheaf unit-restriction iso equals the presheaf
pushforward-unit comparator]
  
  
  
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  Let \(f : Y \hookrightarrow X\) be an open immersion with pullback ring map
  \(\varphi = f.\mathtt{toRingCatSheafHom}\) and pushforward ring map \(\beta\). Applying the
  forgetful functor \(\mathtt{forget} : \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,Y \to \mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\)
  to the composite of the chart-scheme unit identification
  \(\mathtt{restrictFunctorIsoPullback}\,f\) (\cref{def:restrictfunctorisopullback}) at the
  structure-sheaf unit \(\mathbf 1_X\) with the sheaf pullback-unit iso \(\mathtt{pullbackUnitIso}\,f\)
  yields, on the nose, the presheaf pushforward-unit comparator
  \(q = \mathtt{presheafPushforwardUnitIso}\,f\) (\cref{def:presheaf_pushforward_unit_iso}, the inverse
  strong-monoidal unit comparator \(\varepsilon(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta)^{-1}\)):
  \[
    \mathtt{forget}.\mathrm{mapIso}\bigl(
      (\mathtt{restrictFunctorIsoPullback}\,f).\mathrm{app}\,(\mathbf 1_X)
        \;\mathbin{;}\; \mathtt{pullbackUnitIso}\,f\bigr)
      \;=\; \mathtt{presheafPushforwardUnitIso}\,f .
  \]
  This is the leg of the unit assembly (\cref{lem:dual_unit_iso_restrict_assemble}) that closes its
  dual flank.

[RES2 L1: the unit pullback identification factors through \texttt{sheafificationCompPullback} and the
$X$-sheafification counit]
  
  
  
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  Let \(f : Y \hookrightarrow X\) be an open immersion with pullback ring map
  \(\varphi = f.\mathtt{toRingCatSheafHom}\), let \(M_0 = \mathtt{SheafOfModules.unit}\,\mathcal O_X\) be
  the structure-sheaf unit over \(X\) with underlying presheaf
  \(\mathbf 1^{\mathrm p}_X = M_0.\mathrm{val}\), and write
  \(\mathtt{cuX} = (\mathtt{sheafificationAdjunction}\,\mathbf 1_X).\mathrm{counit}\) for the
  $X$-sheafification counit. The per-object pullback identification
  \(\mathtt{pullbackValIso}\,f\,M_0\) (\cref{def:pullback_val_iso}) factors, on the nose, as the
  inverse \(\mathtt{sheafificationCompPullback}\,\varphi\) comparison at \(\mathbf 1^{\mathrm p}_X\)
  followed by the sheaf pullback of \(\mathtt{cuX}\) at \(M_0\):
  \[
    (\mathtt{pullbackValIso}\,f\,M_0).\mathrm{hom}
      = (\mathtt{sheafificationCompPullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{inv}.\mathrm{app}\,\mathbf 1^{\mathrm p}_X
        \;\mathbin{;}\; (\mathtt{pullback}\,f).\mathrm{map}\bigl(\mathtt{cuX}.\mathrm{app}\,M_0\bigr).
  \]

[RES2 L2: the sheafified presheaf pullback-unit comparator reconciles with the sheaf pullback
identification]
  
  
  
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  With notation as above, let \(\mathtt{cuY} = (\mathtt{sheafificationAdjunction}\,\mathbf 1_Y).\mathrm{counit}\)
  be the $Y$-sheafification counit, \(a_Y = \mathtt{sheafification}\,\mathbf 1_Y\), and
  \(p = \mathtt{presheafPullbackUnitIso}\,f\) (\cref{def:presheaf_pullback_unit_iso}, the presheaf
  pullback-unit comparator \(p = (H_1\,\mathbf 1^{\mathrm p}_X)^{-1} \mathbin{;} q\) with \(H_1\) the
  \(\mathtt{leftAdjointUniq}\) factor and \(q = \mathtt{presheafPushforwardUnitIso}\,f\),
  \cref{def:presheaf_pushforward_unit_iso}). Then sheafifying \(p\) and cancelling the
  $Y$-sheafification counit reproduces the sheaf pullback identification followed by the sheaf
  pullback-unit iso \(\mathtt{pullbackUnitIso}\,f\) (\cref{lem:pullback_unit_iso}):
  \[
    a_Y.\mathrm{map}\,(p.\mathrm{hom}) \;\mathbin{;}\; \mathtt{cuY}.\mathrm{app}\,\mathbf 1_Y
      = (\mathtt{pullbackValIso}\,f\,M_0).\mathrm{hom}
        \;\mathbin{;}\; (\mathtt{pullbackUnitIso}\,f).\mathrm{hom}.
  \]
  Both sides are sheaf morphisms
  \(a_Y\bigl((\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi').\mathrm{obj}\,\mathbf 1^{\mathrm p}_X\bigr) \to \mathcal O_Y\).

[$(*)$ / RES2 (pullback unit-only reconciliation): the sheaf pullback-unit comparator sheafifies
the presheaf pullback-unit comparator]
  
  
  
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  Let \(f : Y \hookrightarrow X\) be an open immersion. Pulling back the sheafification counit over
  \(X\) at the structure-sheaf unit and then applying the sheaf pullback-unit iso
  \(\mathtt{pullbackUnitIso}\,f\) equals: the \(\mathtt{sheafificationCompPullback}\) comparison at the
  unit presheaf, then the sheafification of the presheaf pullback-unit comparator
  \(p = \mathtt{presheafPullbackUnitIso}\,f\) (\cref{def:presheaf_pullback_unit_iso}), then the
  sheafification counit over \(Y\) at the unit:
  \[
    (\mathtt{pullback}\,f).\mathrm{map}\bigl(\mathtt{sheafCounit}_X.\mathrm{app}\,\mathbf 1_X\bigr)
      \;\mathbin{;}\; (\mathtt{pullbackUnitIso}\,f).\mathrm{hom}
      \;=\; \mathtt{sheafificationCompPullback}\,\varphi.\mathrm{hom}.\mathrm{app}\,\mathbf 1_X^{\mathrm p}
  \]
  \[
      \qquad\qquad
        \;\mathbin{;}\; \mathtt{sheafification}.\mathrm{map}\bigl((\mathtt{presheafPullbackUnitIso}\,f).\mathrm{hom}\bigr)
        \;\mathbin{;}\; \mathtt{sheafCounit}_Y.\mathrm{app}\,\mathbf 1_Y ,
  \]
  where \(\mathtt{sheafCounit}_\bullet\) is the counit of the sheafification adjunction over
  \(\bullet\) and \(\mathbf 1_X^{\mathrm p}\) the underlying presheaf unit. This is the genuine
  unit-side reconciliation of the dual-unit naturality square; no dual factor remains.

[S4a: dual-unit contraction commutes with further restriction]
  
  
  
  With \(j : V \hookrightarrow U\) as above, the unit-duality contraction
  \(\mathtt{dual\_unit\_iso} : \mathcal{H}om(\mathcal{O}_U, \mathcal{O}_U) \cong \mathcal{O}_U\)
  (\cref{lem:dual_unit_iso}) commutes with further restriction along \(j\). Writing
  \(\mathtt{dual\_unit\_iso}_U\), \(\mathtt{dual\_unit\_iso}_V\) for the contraction over \(U\), \(V\)
  and \(u_j = \mathtt{unitRestrictIso}\,j\) (\cref{def:unitrestrictiso}) for the chart-scheme unit
  identification, the compatibility is the conjugated four-leg identity
  \[
    \mathtt{dual\_unit\_iso}_V
      \;=\; \mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,(u_j)
        \;\mathbin{;}\; \bigl(\mathtt{dual\_restrict\_iso}\,j\,(\mathbf 1_U)\bigr)^{-1}
        \;\mathbin{;}\; (\mathtt{restrictFunctor}\,j).\mathrm{mapIso}\,\mathtt{dual\_unit\_iso}_U
        \;\mathbin{;}\; u_j,
  \]
  where \(\mathbf 1_U\) is the structure-sheaf unit over \(U\), \(\mathtt{dual\_restrict\_iso}\,j\)
  (\cref{lem:dual_restrict_iso}) is the dual-restriction comparison along \(j\), and
  \(\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\) (\cref{def:scheme_modules_dual_iso_of_iso}) is the contravariant transport
  of \(u_j\) on the dual flank.

[The unit contraction is the left unitor at the unit (Cone A coverage)]
  
  
  
  The structure-sheaf unit contraction
  \(\mathtt{tensorObj\_unit\_iso} : \mathcal{O}_X \otimes_X \mathcal{O}_X \cong \mathcal{O}_X\)
  coincides with the left unitor \(\mathtt{tensorObj\_left\_unitor}\) (\cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso})
  evaluated at the module argument \(M = \mathcal{O}_X\).

[Naturality of the left unitor (Cone A coverage)]
  
  
  
  For a morphism \(g : M \to N\) of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules, the left unitor
  (\cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}) is natural:
  \[
    (\mathbf 1_{\mathcal{O}_X} \otimes g) \mathbin{;} (\mathtt{tensorObj\_left\_unitor}\,N).\mathrm{hom}
      = (\mathtt{tensorObj\_left\_unitor}\,M).\mathrm{hom} \mathbin{;} g .
  \]

[$\eta$ mate-identification: the unit iso is the sheafified presheaf unit comparison (Cone A)]
  
  
  
  Let \(f : Y \to X\) be a morphism of schemes and \(\varphi'\) its associated change-of-rings map.
  The sheaf-level unit comparison \(\mathtt{pullbackUnitIso}\,f\) (\cref{lem:pullback_unit_iso}) is the
  sheafification transport of the presheaf oplax unit comparison
  \(\eta\,(\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi')\) (\cref{lem:presheaf_pullback_oplaxmonoidal}):
  \[
    (\mathtt{pullbackUnitIso}\,f).\mathrm{hom}
      = (\mathtt{pullbackValIso}\,f\,\mathcal{O}_X).\mathrm{inv}
        \mathbin{;} a_Y.\mathrm{map}\bigl(\eta\,(\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi')\bigr)
        \mathbin{;} \mathtt{sheafifyUnitIso}.\mathrm{hom},
  \]
  where \(\mathtt{pullbackValIso}\) (\cref{def:pullback_val_iso}) reconciles the sheaf-level pullback
  value with the sheafified presheaf pullback and \(\mathtt{sheafifyUnitIso}\) --- the unit-specific
  sheafification reconciliation isomorphism --- closes the unit (\(\mathbf 1\)) seam.

[$\delta$ mate-identification: the tensor comparison is the sheafified cotensorator (Cone A)]
  
  
  
  Let \(f : Y \to X\) and \(M, N \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\). The sheaf-level tensor comparison
  \(\mathtt{pullbackTensorMap}\,f\,M\,N\) (\cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map}) is the sheafification
  transport of the presheaf oplax cotensorator
  \(\delta\,(\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi')\,M_{\mathrm{val}}\,N_{\mathrm{val}}\)
  (\cref{lem:presheaf_pullback_oplaxmonoidal}):
  \[
    \mathtt{pullbackTensorMap}\,f\,M\,N
      = (\mathtt{pullbackValIso}\,f\,(M \otimes_X N)).\mathrm{inv}
        \mathbin{;} a_Y.\mathrm{map}\bigl(\delta\,(\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi')\,M_{\mathrm{val}}\,N_{\mathrm{val}}\bigr)
        \mathbin{;} \beta_{M,N},
  \]
  where \(\beta_{M,N}\) is the tensor-of-\(\mathtt{pullbackValIso}\) reconciliation
  (\cref{def:pullback_val_iso}, \cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}) on the \(\delta\)-codomain,
  identifying the \(a_Y\)-image of the presheaf tensor with \(f^{*}M \otimes_Y f^{*}N\).

[Cone A sub-lemma 1: \texttt{pullbackValIso} naturality at the sheaf left unitor (RHS reconciliation)]
  
  
  
  Let \(f : Y \to X\) and \(M \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\); write
  \(F = \mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi'\) for the presheaf-level change-of-rings pullback, \(a_Y\) for the
  sheafification functor on \(Y\), and \(\mathtt{pbv}_{(-)} = \mathtt{pullbackValIso}\,f\,(-)\)
  (\cref{def:pullback_val_iso}). The reconciliation isomorphism \(\mathtt{pullbackValIso}\,f\) is
  natural; instantiated against the sheaf-level left unitor
  \((\mathtt{tensorObj\_left\_unitor}\,M).\mathrm{hom} : \mathcal{O}_X \otimes M \to M\), its naturality
  square identifies the \(f^{*}\)-image of that unitor --- the right-hand side of the identity of
  \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map_left_unitality} --- as the \(a_Y\)-sheafification of the
  \(F\)-image of the unitor's underlying presheaf morphism, conjugated by \(\mathtt{pbv}\) on source
  and target:
  \[
    (\mathtt{pbv}_{\mathcal{O}_X \otimes M}).\mathrm{hom}
      \mathbin{;} f^{*}\bigl((\mathtt{tensorObj\_left\_unitor}\,M).\mathrm{hom}\bigr)
    = a_Y.\mathrm{map}\bigl(F.\mathrm{map}\,(\mathtt{tensorObj\_left\_unitor}\,M).\mathrm{hom}_{\mathrm{val}}\bigr)
      \mathbin{;} (\mathtt{pbv}_{M}).\mathrm{hom},
  \]
  where \((-)_{\mathrm{val}}\) denotes the underlying presheaf morphism (the forget-image) of a sheaf
  morphism.

\textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
  Let \(L : \mathcal{C} \to \mathcal{D}\) be a localization functor for a monoidal morphism property
  \(W\), so that \(\mathcal{D} = \mathtt{LocalizedMonoidal}\,L\,W\,\varepsilon\) carries the transported
  (Gabriel--Zisman / Day-style) monoidal structure, with unit comparison \(\varepsilon\) and tensor
  comparison \(\mu_{X,Y} : L X \otimes L Y \cong L(X \otimes Y)\). For every object \(Y\) of
  \(\mathcal{C}\), the localized left unitor at the image object \(L Y\) collapses to the \(L\)-image of
  the source left unitor, modulo the unit and tensor comparisons:
  \[
    (\lambda_{L Y}).\mathrm{hom}
      = \varepsilon^{-1} \rhd L Y
        \mathbin{;} (\mu_{\mathbf 1,\,Y}).\mathrm{hom}
        \mathbin{;} L.\mathrm{map}\,(\lambda_Y).\mathrm{hom},
  \]
  where \(\mathbf 1\) is the source tensor unit. The proof is definitional: the localized left unitor
  is constructed as a \(\mathtt{Localization.liftNatIso}\) of the source unitor, so on an image object it
  reduces by \cref{lem:localization_lift_nat_trans_app} to the source datum conjugated by the lifting
  isomorphisms, with no further coherence obligations.
nodes.title: Localization-monoidal left-unitor collapse

nodes.statement:
\textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
  Let \(L : \mathcal{C} \to \mathcal{D}\) be a localization functor for \(W\), let \(F_1, F_2 :
  \mathcal{C} \to \mathcal{E}\) be functors that lift to \(F_1', F_2' : \mathcal{D} \to \mathcal{E}\)
  along \(L\), and let \(\tau : F_1 \to F_2\). The unique lifted natural transformation
  \(\mathtt{liftNatTrans}\,\tau : F_1' \to F_2'\), evaluated at an image object \(L X\), is the source
  datum conjugated by the lifting isomorphisms:
  \[
    (\mathtt{liftNatTrans}\,\tau)_{L X}
      = (\mathtt{Lifting.iso}\,F_1')_{X}.\mathrm{hom}
        \mathbin{;} \tau_X
        \mathbin{;} (\mathtt{Lifting.iso}\,F_2')_{X}.\mathrm{inv}.
  \]
  Thus a coherence between lifted transformations, tested on objects in the image of \(L\), reduces
  definitionally to the corresponding coherence of the source data pinned by the lifting iso --- the
  mechanism underlying the localization-monoidal unitor collapse above.
nodes.title: Evaluation of a lifted natural transformation on an image object

nodes.statement:
\textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
  Let \(\alpha : R_0 \to R.\mathrm{obj}\) be the comparison from a presheaf of rings to its
  sheafification (locally injective and locally surjective). The sheafification reflector
  \(a = \mathtt{PresheafOfModules.sheafification}\,\alpha : \mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,R_0 \to
  \mathtt{SheafOfModules}\,R\) is a localization functor for the morphism class
  \(W = J.W.\mathtt{inverseImage}\,(\mathtt{toPresheaf}\,R_0)\) of presheaf morphisms inverted by
  sheafification. Consequently the entire \(\mathtt{Localization.Monoidal}\) API ---
  \cref{lem:localization_monoidal_left_unitor_hom_app} and
  \cref{lem:localization_lift_nat_trans_app} in particular --- is applicable to \(a\).
nodes.title: The sheafification of presheaves of modules is a localization

nodes.statement:
[STU-collapse: the sheaf left unitor at a pullback image object]
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  Let \(f : Y \to X\), write \(a = a_Y = \mathtt{PresheafOfModules.sheafification}\) for the
  sheafification reflector on \(Y\), \(\varepsilon = \mathtt{sheafifyUnitIso}\) for its unit comparison
  and \(\mu = \mathtt{sheafifyTensorUnitIso}\) for its tensor comparison, and \(F = \mathtt{pullback}\,
  \varphi'\) for the presheaf-level change-of-rings pullback. For the image object
  \(a.\mathrm{obj}\,(F.\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}})\) --- the sheafification of the presheaf pullback
  of \(M\) --- the sheaf-level left unitor collapses, modulo \(\varepsilon\) and \(\mu\), to the
  sheafified presheaf left unitor:
  \[
    \bigl(\mathtt{tensorObj\_left\_unitor}\,(a.\mathrm{obj}\,(F.\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}}))\bigr).\mathrm{hom}
      = \varepsilon^{-1} \rhd a.\mathrm{obj}\,(F.\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}})
        \mathbin{;} (\mu_{\mathbf 1,\,F.\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}}}).\mathrm{hom}
        \mathbin{;} a.\mathrm{map}\,\bigl(\lambda_{F.\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}}}\bigr).
  \]
  This is the shared structural residual of \cref{lem:tensorobj_left_unitor_pullback_eq_sheafify} and
  of the central-leg sub-step of
  \cref{lem:pullback_unit_iso_whisker_eq_sheafify_eta_whisker}.

[Cone A sub-lemma 2: the pullback-object left unitor is the sheafified presheaf unitor]
  
  
  
  With notation as above, write \(\mathtt{pbv}_{(-)} = \mathtt{pullbackValIso}\,f\,(-)\) and let
  \[
    W_{\lambda}
      = (\mathtt{sheafifyTensorUnitIso}_{\mathbf 1,\,F.\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}}}).\mathrm{hom}
        \mathbin{;} a_Y.\mathrm{map}\bigl(\mathtt{sheafifyUnitIso} \otimes (\mathtt{pbv}\,M)\bigr)
  \]
  be the \emph{unit-reconciled} \((\mathbf 1,\,f^{*}M)\)-wrapper: its tensor unit is the presheaf unit
  \(\mathbf 1 = \mathbf 1_{\mathtt{PSh}}\) and its left tensor leg is the unit comparison
  \(\mathtt{sheafifyUnitIso}\) (\emph{not} the \(\delta\)-wrapper \(W\) of
  \cref{lem:pullback_unit_iso_whisker_eq_sheafify_eta_whisker}, whose unit is \(F\,\mathbf 1\) and whose
  left leg is \(\mathtt{pbv}\,\mathcal{O}_X\)). Post-composed with the sheaf-level left unitor at the
  pullback object \(f^{*}M\), \(W_{\lambda}\) equals the sheafified presheaf left unitor of
  \(F.\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}}\), conjugated by \(\mathtt{pullbackValIso}\) on the target:
  \[
    (\mathtt{sheafifyTensorUnitIso}_{\mathbf 1,\,F.\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}}}).\mathrm{hom}
      \mathbin{;} a_Y.\mathrm{map}\bigl(\mathtt{sheafifyUnitIso} \otimes (\mathtt{pbv}\,M)\bigr)
      \mathbin{;} (\mathtt{tensorObj\_left\_unitor}\,(f^{*}M)).\mathrm{hom}
    = a_Y.\mathrm{map}\bigl(\lambda_{F.\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}}}\bigr)
      \mathbin{;} (\mathtt{pbv}_{M}).\mathrm{hom},
  \]
  where \(\mathbf 1 = \mathbf 1_{\mathtt{PSh}}\) is the presheaf tensor unit. The unit-reconciled
  wrapper \(W_{\lambda}\) is precisely the right-hand tail produced by the
  \(\delta\)-side \(\eta\)-whisker identity
  \cref{lem:pullback_unit_iso_whisker_eq_sheafify_eta_whisker}; that lemma performs the
  \(W \to W_{\lambda}\) handoff that converts the \(\delta\)-wrapper into the unit-reconciled wrapper
  consumed here.

[Cone A sub-lemma 3: the whiskered unit comparison is the sheafified \(\eta\)-whisker]
  
  
  
  With notation as above, write \(\mathtt{pbv}_{(-)} = \mathtt{pullbackValIso}\,f\,(-)\),
  \(\eta\,F = \mathtt{Functor.OplaxMonoidal.}\eta\,(\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi')\) for the presheaf
  oplax unit (\cref{lem:presheaf_pullback_oplaxmonoidal}), and let
  \begin{align*}
    W &= (\mathtt{sheafifyTensorUnitIso}_{F.\mathrm{obj}\,\mathbf 1,\,F.\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}}}).\mathrm{hom}
      \mathbin{;} a_Y.\mathrm{map}\bigl((\mathtt{pbv}\,\mathcal{O}_X)\otimes(\mathtt{pbv}\,M)\bigr),\\
    W_{\lambda} &= (\mathtt{sheafifyTensorUnitIso}_{\mathbf 1,\,F.\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}}}).\mathrm{hom}
      \mathbin{;} a_Y.\mathrm{map}\bigl(\mathtt{sheafifyUnitIso}\otimes(\mathtt{pbv}\,M)\bigr)
  \end{align*}
  be respectively the \(\delta\)-identification right wrapper of
  \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map_eq_sheafify_delta} (tensor unit \(F\,\mathbf 1\), left leg
  \(\mathtt{pbv}\,\mathcal{O}_X\)) and the unit-reconciled wrapper of
  \cref{lem:tensorobj_left_unitor_pullback_eq_sheafify} (tensor unit \(\mathbf 1 =
  \mathbf 1_{\mathtt{PSh}}\), left leg \(\mathtt{sheafifyUnitIso}\)). Then the wrapper \(W\),
  post-composed with the unit comparison whiskered into the left tensor factor, equals the sheafified
  presheaf \(\eta\)-whisker followed by the unit-reconciled wrapper \(W_{\lambda}\):
  \[
    W \mathbin{;}
    (\mathtt{tensorObjIsoOfIso}\,(\mathtt{pullbackUnitIso}\,f)\,\mathbf 1_{f^{*}M}).\mathrm{hom}
    = a_Y.\mathrm{map}\bigl(\eta\,F \rhd F.\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}}\bigr)
      \mathbin{;} W_{\lambda}.
  \]
  This is the \(W \to W_{\lambda}\) handoff: it converts the \(\delta\)-wrapper into the unit-reconciled
  wrapper consumed by \cref{lem:tensorobj_left_unitor_pullback_eq_sheafify}, at the cost of the
  \(\eta\)-whisker prefix.

[Sheaf-level left unitality of the pullback tensor comparison (Cone A core)]
  
  
  
  Let \(f : Y \to X\) and \(M \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\). The sheaf-level pullback functor
  \(f^{*} = \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules.pullback}\,f\) satisfies left unitality: the composite of the
  tensor comparison on the unit pair, the unit comparison whiskered into the left factor, and the
  target left unitor equals the \(f^{*}\)-image of the source left unitor,
  \[
    \mathtt{pullbackTensorMap}\,f\,\mathcal{O}_X\,M
      \mathbin{;} \bigl((\mathtt{pullbackUnitIso}\,f) \otimes \mathbf 1_{f^{*}M}\bigr)
      \mathbin{;} (\mathtt{tensorObj\_left\_unitor}\,(f^{*}M)).\mathrm{hom}
    = f^{*}\bigl((\mathtt{tensorObj\_left\_unitor}\,M).\mathrm{hom}\bigr).
  \]

[S4b inner seam: pullback-world left-unitor coherence at the chart unit (Cone A)]
  
  
  
  With \(j : V \hookrightarrow U\) the chart morphism (\(j \mathbin{;} \iota_U = \iota_V\)), the
  \emph{inner} seam of S4b is the pullback-world image of the left-unitor coherence at the chart unit.
  Transporting the restriction \((\mathtt{restrict}\,j)\,(\mathtt{tensorObj\_unit\_iso})\) through the
  chart comparison \(\mathtt{restrictFunctorIsoPullback}\,j\) (\cref{def:restrictfunctorisopullback})
  and promoting the tensor leg by Bridge~B1
  (\cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso_eq_pullback_tensor_map}), the square reduces to the sheaf-level
  left unitality \(\mathtt{pullbackTensorMap\_left\_unitality}\,j\)
  (\cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map_left_unitality}) evaluated at \(M = \mathcal{O}_U\), with the unit leg
  supplied by \(\mathtt{unitRestrictIso}\,j\) (\cref{def:unitrestrictiso}) and
  \(\mathtt{pullbackUnitIso}\,j\) (\cref{lem:pullback_unit_iso}).

[S4b: unit left-unitor commutes with further restriction]
  
  
  
  With \(j : V \hookrightarrow U\) as above, the unit-tensor contraction
  \(\mathtt{tensorObj\_unit\_iso} : \mathcal{O}_U \otimes_U (-) \cong (-)\) (the left unitor at the
  unit, \cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}) commutes with further restriction along \(j\): modulo the
  reindexing \(\varrho\),
  \[
    (\mathtt{restrict}\,j)\,(\mathtt{tensorObj\_unit\_iso}\ \text{over}\ U)
      \;=\; \mathtt{tensorObj\_unit\_iso}\ \text{over}\ V.
  \]
  Unlike the tensor-restriction comparison \(\mathtt{tensorObj\_restrict\_iso}\), the contraction
  \(\mathtt{tensorObj\_unit\_iso}\) is \emph{not} built from the abstract pullback: by definition it
  is the \emph{sheafification of the presheaf-level left unitor at the unit}, followed by the
  sheafification-adjunction counit (\cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}). Bridge B1 does not apply to the
  contraction directly; instead the square reduces, via the chart comparison
  \(\mathtt{restrictFunctorIsoPullback}\), to a pullback-world left-unitality coherence (Cone~A,
  \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map_left_unitality}), established below.

[S4c: global-unit comparison \(u_\iota\) commutes with further restriction]
  
  
  
  With \(j : V \hookrightarrow U\) as above, the global-unit comparison
  \[
    u_\iota(f) := \bigl(\mathtt{restrictFunctorIsoPullback}\,f\bigr).\mathrm{app}\,
      (\mathtt{SheafOfModules.unit}\,X) \mathbin{;} \mathtt{pullbackUnitIso}\,f
      : \mathtt{restrict}\,(\mathcal{O}_X)\,f \cong \mathcal{O}_U
  \]
  (\cref{lem:pullback_unit_iso}), whose inverse \(u_\iota(f)^{-1}\) is the final leg of the
  trivialisation chain, commutes with further restriction along \(j\): modulo the reindexing
  \(\varrho\),
  \[
    (\mathtt{restrict}\,j)\,u_\iota(\iota_U)^{-1} \;=\; u_\iota(\iota_V)^{-1}.
  \]

[RFIP pseudonaturality across an equality of open immersions]
  
  
  
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  Let \(f, f' : Y \hookrightarrow X\) be open immersions with \(h : f = f'\), and let
  \(A \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\). The restriction--pullback comparison
  \(\mathtt{restrictFunctorIsoPullback}\) (\cref{def:restrictfunctorisopullback}) is pseudonatural
  across the equality \(h\): the comparison at \(f\) followed by the \(\mathtt{pullbackCongr}\,h\)
  reindex equals the \(\mathtt{restrictFunctorCongr}\,h\) reindex followed by the comparison at
  \(f'\),
  \[
    (\mathtt{restrictFunctorIsoPullback}\,f).\mathrm{hom}.\mathrm{app}\,A
      \mathbin{;} (\mathtt{pullbackCongr}\,h).\mathrm{hom}.\mathrm{app}\,A
    = (\mathtt{restrictFunctorCongr}\,h).\mathrm{hom}.\mathrm{app}\,A
      \mathbin{;} (\mathtt{restrictFunctorIsoPullback}\,f').\mathrm{hom}.\mathrm{app}\,A.
  \]
  This is the leaf reindex helper threaded by the Seam-1 keystone
  (\cref{lem:restrict_iso_unit_of_le_eq_restrict}) to push the leading comparison onto the literal
  composite chart morphism.

[Seam 1 keystone: the unit-of-\(\le\) restriction iso is the chart restriction of \(e^M\)]
  
  
  
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  Let \(j : V \hookrightarrow U\) be the chart morphism (\(j \mathbin{;} \iota_U = \iota_V\)) and
  \(e^M : M|_{\iota_U} \cong \mathcal{O}_U\) a trivialisation. Then the unit-of-\(\le\) restriction
  isomorphism factors through the chart restriction of \(e^M\):
  \[
    \mathtt{restrictIsoUnitOfLE}\,h_{VU}\,e^M
      \;=\; \mathtt{restrictCompReindex}\,j\,M
        \mathbin{;} (\mathtt{restrictFunctor}\,j).\mathrm{mapIso}\,e^M
        \mathbin{;} \mathtt{unitRestrictIso}\,j,
  \]
  where \(\mathtt{restrictCompReindex}\,j\,M\) is the restriction-composite reindexing \(\varrho_M\)
  (\cref{def:restrictcompreindex}) and \(\mathtt{unitRestrictIso}\,j\) the unit-restriction
  identification \(u_\iota(j)\) (\cref{def:unitrestrictiso}).

[Telescope seam 1, Step A: \(e^M\)-bifunctoriality split of constituent (2)]
  
  
  
  
  
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  Let \(j : V \hookrightarrow U\) be the chart morphism (\(j \mathbin{;} \iota_U = \iota_V\)) and
  \(e^M : L|_{\iota_U} \cong \mathcal O_U\) a trivialisation. Write
  \(c = \mathtt{dual\_restrict\_iso}\,\iota_U \mathbin{;} (\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,e^M)^{-1}
  \mathbin{;} \mathtt{dual\_unit\_iso}\) for the wrapped \(U\)-side dual chain. Then \(\mathtt{restrict}\,j\)
  carries the constituent-(2) comparison \(\mathtt{tensorObjIsoOfIso}\,e^M\,c\) past the
  tensor-restriction comparison \(\mathtt{tensorObj\_restrict\_iso}\,j\) on both flanks:
  \[
    (\mathtt{restrictFunctor}\,j).\mathrm{mapIso}\,(\mathtt{tensorObjIsoOfIso}\,e^M\,c)
      = \mathtt{tensorObj\_restrict\_iso}\,j\,(L|_{\iota_U})\,((\mathtt{dual}\,L)|_{\iota_U})
        \mathbin{;} \mathtt{tensorObjIsoOfIso}\,
            ((\mathtt{restrictFunctor}\,j).\mathrm{mapIso}\,e^M)\,
            ((\mathtt{restrictFunctor}\,j).\mathrm{mapIso}\,c)
        \mathbin{;} (\mathtt{tensorObj\_restrict\_iso}\,j\,\mathcal O_U\,\mathcal O_U)^{-1}.
  \]

[Telescope seam 1, Step B: the \(e^M\)-leg refined to the unit-of-\(\le\) restriction iso]
  
  
  
  
  
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  With \(j : V \hookrightarrow U\), \(h_{VU} : V \le U\) and \(e^M : L|_{\iota_U} \cong \mathcal O_U\),
  write \(\varrho_L = \mathtt{restrictCompReindex}\,j\,L\) (\cref{def:restrictcompreindex}) and
  \(u_j = \mathtt{unitRestrictIso}\,j\) (\cref{def:unitrestrictiso}). The \(\mathtt{restrict}\,j\)-image of
  the trivialisation \(e^M\) is the unit-of-\(\le\) restriction iso conjugated by \(\varrho_L\) and
  \(u_j\):
  \[
    (\mathtt{restrictFunctor}\,j).\mathrm{mapIso}\,e^M
      = \varrho_L^{-1} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{restrictIsoUnitOfLE}\,h_{VU}\,e^M \mathbin{;} u_j^{-1}.
  \]

[Telescope seam 1, Step C: the dual-chain leg, distributed and identified leg by leg]
  
  
  
  
  
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  Keep \(j, h_{VU}, e^M\) as above. Write
  \(c = \mathtt{dual\_restrict\_iso}\,\iota_U \mathbin{;} (\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,e^M)^{-1}
  \mathbin{;} \mathtt{dual\_unit\_iso}_U\) for the \(U\)-built dual chain and
  \[
    c_V = \mathtt{dual\_restrict\_iso}\,\iota_V
      \mathbin{;} \bigl(\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,(\mathtt{restrictIsoUnitOfLE}\,h_{VU}\,e^M)\bigr)^{-1}
      \mathbin{;} \mathtt{dual\_unit\_iso}_V
  \]
  for its \(V\)-built refinement (with \(e^M\) replaced by \(\mathtt{restrictIsoUnitOfLE}\,h_{VU}\,e^M\)).
  Writing \(\varrho_{\mathtt{dual}\,L} = \mathtt{restrictCompReindex}\,j\,(\mathtt{dual}\,L)\) and
  \(u_j = \mathtt{unitRestrictIso}\,j\), the \(\mathtt{restrict}\,j\)-image of the dual chain is its
  \(V\)-built refinement conjugated by \(\varrho_{\mathtt{dual}\,L}\) and \(u_j\):
  \[
    (\mathtt{restrictFunctor}\,j).\mathrm{mapIso}\,c
      = \varrho_{\mathtt{dual}\,L}^{-1} \mathbin{;} c_V \mathbin{;} u_j^{-1}.
  \]

[Telescope seam 1: \(e^M\)-bifunctoriality split of the wrapped dual constituent]
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  With \(j : V \hookrightarrow U\) the chart morphism (\(j \mathbin{;} \iota_U = \iota_V\)) and
  \(e^M : L|_{\iota_U} \cong \mathcal{O}_U\) a trivialisation, applying \(\mathtt{restrict}\,j\) to the
  \(U\)-side constituent
  \(\mathtt{tensorObjIsoOfIso}\,e^M\,\bigl(\mathtt{dual\_restrict\_iso}\,\iota_U \mathbin{;}
  (\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,e^M)^{-1} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{dual\_unit\_iso}\bigr)\) splits, by
  module-naturality of the tensor iso-of-iso comparison
  (\cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso_natural}), into:
  \begin{itemize}
    \item the \(e^M\)-leg, whose \(V\)-refinement is the identification
      \(\mathtt{restrictIsoUnitOfLE}\,h_{VU}\,e^M = (\mathtt{restrict}\,j)\,e^M\)
      (\cref{lem:restrict_iso_unit_of_le_eq_restrict}, \cref{def:unitrestrictiso}); and
    \item the dual-chain leg, distributed leg by leg across \(\mathtt{restrict}\,j\): the bare
      dual-restriction comparison covered by S3 (\cref{lem:dual_restrict_iso_restrict_compat}), the
      contravariant \((\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,e^M)^{-1}\) (\cref{def:scheme_modules_dual_iso_of_iso})
      transported by module-naturality of \(\mathtt{dual\_restrict\_iso}\)
      (\cref{lem:dual_restrict_iso_natural}) into \((\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,((\mathtt{restrict}\,j)\,e^M))^{-1}\),
      and \(\mathtt{dual\_unit\_iso}\) covered by S4a (\cref{lem:dual_unit_iso_restrict_compat}).
  \end{itemize}
  Concretely, modulo the reindexing \(\varrho = \mathtt{restrictCompReindex}\,j\)
  (\cref{def:restrictcompreindex}), the \(\mathtt{restrict}\,j\)-image of constituent (2) equals the
  \(V\)-built \(\mathtt{tensorObjIsoOfIso}\,(\mathtt{restrictIsoUnitOfLE}\,h_{VU}\,e^M)\,(\cdots_V)\)
  conjugated by \(\varrho\) on both flanks.

[Telescope seam 2: generic \(\varrho\)-cancellation assembly of the five squares]
  
  
  
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  Work in an arbitrary category \(C\). Given the five \(U\)-built constituents \(c^U_1, \dots, c^U_5\)
  of the trivialisation chain and their \(V\)-built counterparts \(c^V_1, \dots, c^V_5\), the four
  internal reindexings \(\varrho_1, \dots, \varrho_4\) (the
  \(\mathtt{restrictCompReindex}\,j\)/\(\mathtt{unitRestrictIso}\) factors of
  \cref{def:restrictcompreindex}), and the two outer reindexings \(\varrho_0, \varrho_5\), suppose each
  constituent satisfies its restriction-naturality square
  \[
    (\mathtt{restrict}\,j)\,c^U_k \;=\; \varrho_{k-1}^{-1} \mathbin{;} c^V_k \mathbin{;} \varrho_k
    \qquad (k = 1, \dots, 5),
  \]
  so that the target \(\varrho_k\) of square \(k\) is the source \(\varrho_k\) of square \(k+1\). Then,
  composing the five squares in order, the internal reindexings \(\varrho_1, \dots, \varrho_4\) cancel
  telescopically and
  \[
    (\mathtt{restrict}\,j)\bigl(c^U_1 \mathbin{;} \cdots \mathbin{;} c^U_5\bigr)
      \;=\; \varrho_0^{-1} \mathbin{;} \bigl(c^V_1 \mathbin{;} \cdots \mathbin{;} c^V_5\bigr) \mathbin{;} \varrho_5,
  \]
  leaving only the two outer reindexings \(\varrho_0 = \mathtt{hobjU}\) and \(\varrho_5 = \mathtt{hobjV}\).

[Telescope seam 3: sectionwise evaluation of the two outer reindexings]
  
  
  
  Let \(\mathtt{hobjU} = \mathtt{image\_preimage\_of\_le}\,U\,h_{VU}\) and
  \(\mathtt{hobjV} = \mathtt{image\_preimage\_of\_le}\,V\,\mathrm{le\_rfl}\) be the two direct-image
  identities of \cref{lem:image_preimage_of_le}, with images \(\iota_U\,''^U(\iota_U^{-1}(V)) = V\) and
  \(\iota_V\,''^V(\iota_V^{-1}(V)) = V\). Threading the corresponding \(\mathtt{eqToHom}\) transports
  through the two flanks of the telescoped chain (\cref{lem:trivialisation_telescope_assemble}) and
  evaluating \(\mathtt{.val.presheaf.map}\)/\(\mathtt{.val.app}\) sectionwise over the preimage open
  \(\iota_U^{-1}(V)\) yields precisely the asserted equality of
  \cref{lem:trivialisation_restrict_compat}.

[Trivialisation iso-chain compatible with further restriction (cocycle residual A, step 1)]
  
  
  
  Let \(V \subseteq U\) be opens of \(X\) (an inclusion of trivialising opens, e.g.
  \(V \subseteq U_i \cap U_j\)). The trivialisation iso-chain that builds the local
  contraction --- the restriction-compatibility iso \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}
  carrying \((L \otimes_X L^{-1})|_U\) to \(\mathcal{O}_U \otimes_U \mathcal{O}_U\),
  composed with the unit contraction \cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso} --- is \emph{natural in
  the open}: its further restriction along \(V \hookrightarrow U\) equals the same chain
  built directly over \(V\). Consequently the sectionwise overlap equation of two local
  contractions \(f_i, f_j\) restricted to \(V\) lifts to an equation of \emph{restricted
  sheaf morphisms} over \(V\), the form on which the iso-level functoriality laws
  (\cref{lem:tensorobjisoofiso_trans}, \cref{lem:dualisoofiso_trans}) and
  \cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_self_duality_collapse} can act.

[Mutually-inverse unit endomorphisms cancel against the left unitor]
  
  
  In any monoidal category \(\mathcal C\), if \(s, s' : \mathbf 1 \to \mathbf 1\) are endomorphisms
  of the monoidal unit with \(s \mathbin{;} s' = \mathbf 1\), then
  \[
    (s \otimes_{\mathrm m} s') \;\mathbin{;}\; \lambda_{\mathbf 1} = \lambda_{\mathbf 1},
  \]
  where \(\lambda_{\mathbf 1} : \mathbf 1 \otimes \mathbf 1 \to \mathbf 1\) is the left unitor at the
  unit.

[Pairing mutually-inverse unit autos through $\mathtt{tensorObjIsoOfIso}$ contracts (cocycle residual A, step 2b)]
  
  
  
  Let \(t, s\) be self-isomorphisms of the trivial line bundle \(\mathcal O_V\) with
  \(t.\mathrm{hom} \mathbin{;} s.\mathrm{hom} = \mathbf 1\). Then pairing them through the
  iso-transport bifunctor and contracting via the unit comparison is trivial:
  \[
    \mathtt{tensorObjIsoOfIso}\,t\,s \;\mathbin{;}\; \mathtt{tensorObj\_unit\_iso}
      = \mathtt{tensorObj\_unit\_iso}.
  \]

Let \(\hat s\) be a presheaf-level automorphism of the unit \(\mathbf 1\) (the underlying map of a
  sheaf-level unit automorphism \(s\)). The presheaf dual-of-unit isomorphism
  \(\mathtt{presheafDualUnitIso} : \mathtt{dual}\,\mathbf 1 \cong \mathbf 1\) is natural in
  \(\hat s\) for the contravariant transport on the source:
  \[
    \mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,\hat s \;\mathbin{;}\; \mathtt{presheafDualUnitIso}
      = \mathtt{presheafDualUnitIso} \;\mathbin{;}\; \hat s.
  \]
nodes.title: Naturality of the presheaf dual-of-unit iso against a unit automorphism (B1 eval-core)

nodes.statement:
[Dual of a unit automorphism is itself (cocycle residual A, step 2a / B1)]
  
  
  
  Let \(s\) be a self-isomorphism of the trivial line bundle \(\mathcal O_V\). Conjugating its
  contravariant dual-transport by the unit-self-duality iso recovers \(s\):
  \[
    \mathtt{dual\_unit\_iso}^{-1}
      \;\mathbin{;}\; \mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,s
      \;\mathbin{;}\; \mathtt{dual\_unit\_iso}
    \;=\; s .
  \]
  Equivalently, the sheaf-level dual of a unit automorphism, read through
  \(\mathtt{dual}\,\mathcal O_V \cong \mathcal O_V\), is the automorphism itself.

[Unit self-duality evaluation collapse (cocycle residual A, step 2)]
  
  
  
  Let \(t\) be a self-isomorphism of the trivial line bundle \(\mathcal{O}_V\) on a
  trivialising open \(V\). Pairing \(t\) on the \(M\)-leg with the dual-transported transition
  on the \(N\)-leg --- which lands at the node \(\mathtt{dual}\,\mathcal{O}_V\) and so must be
  conjugated back to \(\mathcal{O}_V\) through the unit-self-duality iso
  \(\mathtt{dual\_unit\_iso} : \mathtt{dual}\,\mathcal{O}_V \cong \mathcal{O}_V\)
  (\cref{lem:dual_unit_iso}) --- and then contracting via the unit comparison cancels:
  \[
    \mathtt{tensorObjIsoOfIso}\,t\,
      \bigl(\mathtt{dual\_unit\_iso}^{-1}
            \;\mathbin{;}\;(\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,t)^{-1}
            \;\mathbin{;}\;\mathtt{dual\_unit\_iso}\bigr)
    \;\mathbin{;}\;
    \mathtt{tensorObj\_unit\_iso}
    \;=\;
    \mathtt{tensorObj\_unit\_iso}.
  \]
  This is the \(g_{ij}\) (inside \(e^M_x\)) meeting \(g_{ij}^{-1} = \mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,
  g_{ij}\) (inside \(e^N_x\)) cancellation: under the canonical evaluation of the free
  rank-one module against its dual, the transition iso and its inverse-transpose annihilate,
  leaving the trivialisation-independent unit contraction. The \(\mathtt{dual\_unit\_iso}\)
  conjugation on the \(N\)-leg is what makes both factors live on \(\mathcal{O}_V\) so the
  bifunctor \(\mathtt{tensorObjIsoOfIso}\) can pair them; under the eval-at-\(1\) semantics of
  \(\mathtt{presheafDualUnitIso}\) the conjugated dual leg reduces to \(t^{-1}\), so the pair is
  the symmetric \(t \otimes t^{-1}\) that the multiplication kills.

Should the slice-and-sheafify route of
  \cref{def:presheaf_internal_hom,lem:internal_hom_isSheaf} bottom out, the dual can
  instead be assembled by \emph{object-level descent}: glue the local trivial duals
  \(\mathcal{O}_{U_i}\) along the inverse-transpose transition isomorphisms
  \(g_{ij}^{-\mathsf{T}}\) of \(L\) into a global \(L^{-1}\) on \(X\). Abstractly this
  is effective descent for sheaves of modules on the small Zariski site --- the
  essential-surjectivity of the descent-data functor of an \(\mathtt{IsStack}\)
  pseudofunctor \(U \mapsto (\mathcal{O}_U\text{-modules})\). This is the more
  principled and reusable construction but the larger build (it requires constructing
  the restriction pseudofunctor and proving the stack/effective-descent property),
  and is recorded only as a fallback; the slice internal-hom route above is the
  intended one. It is the same morphism-/object-level descent family on which the
  associator re-route of \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso} also waits.
nodes.title: Alternative route via object-level descent (fallback)

nodes.statement:
[Canonical ring map from a terminal object]
  
  
  
  For a presheaf of commutative rings \(R\) on a base category with terminal object
  \(T\) and an object \(Y\), the unique morphism \(Y \to T\) induces the ring map
  \(R(T) \to R(Y)\) along which a global scalar acts on the value at \(Y\).

[Naturality of the terminal ring map]
  
  
  
  The restriction map \(R(g) : R(X) \to R(Y)\) along \(g : X \to Y\) carries
  \(\mathtt{termRingMap}_X\,f\) to \(\mathtt{termRingMap}_Y\,f\), so the images of a
  global scalar \(f \in R(T)\) are compatible with restriction.

[Multiplication by a global scalar as an endomorphism]
  
  
  
  For \(f \in R(T)\), the endomorphism \(m_f^{N} : N \to N\) of a presheaf of modules
  \(N\) that at each object \(Y\) is multiplication by \(\mathtt{termRingMap}_Y\,f \in
  R(Y)\); its naturality is the heart of the global-scalar action on morphism modules.

[Evaluation functional of a dual section]
  
  
  
  At an object \(X\), the \(R(X)\)-linear map \(M(X) \to R(X)\) obtained by evaluating
  a dual section \(\varphi\) at its terminal component.

[Lax-monoidal unit of restrict-scalars]
  
  
  
  The lax-monoidal unit \(\varepsilon\) of \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\,\alpha\),
  assembled sectionwise from the \(\mathbf{ModuleCat}\)-level lax units.

[Lax-monoidal tensorator of restrict-scalars]
  
  
  
  The lax-monoidal tensorator \(\mu\) of \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\,\alpha\),
  assembled sectionwise from the \(\mathbf{ModuleCat}\)-level lax tensorators.

[Underlying \(\mathrm{Ab}\)-presheaf of the internal hom]
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], Modules on Ringed Spaces, \S Internal Hom.}
  The underlying abelian-group-valued presheaf of the internal hom
  \(\mathcal{H}om(M, N)\): to each open \(U\) it assigns the group of
  \(\mathcal{O}|_U\)-linear maps \(M|_U \to N|_U\), with further-restriction as the
  presheaf maps. This is the \(\mathrm{Ab}\)-level part of
  \cref{def:presheaf_internal_hom}; the \(R(U)\)-module refinement is supplied by the
  global scalar action \cref{def:global_smul}.

[Open-by-open evaluation pairing of the internal hom]
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  
  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], Modules on Ringed Spaces, \S Internal Hom.}
  At each object \(X\) the \(R(X)\)-bilinear contraction \(M(X) \otimes_{R(X)}
  M^\vee(X) \to R(X)\), \(s \otimes \varphi \mapsto \varphi(s)\), evaluating a dual
  section on a module section. Assembling these over all opens gives the evaluation
  pairing \(M \otimes M^\vee \to \mathcal{O}\) of \cref{lem:internal_hom_eval}; it is the
  substrate of the tensor--hom counit used to invert an invertible module.

\textit{Source: [Stacks Project], Algebra, Lemma~\texttt{lemma-adjoint-tensor-restrict}
  (Tag 05DQ); the sheaf/ringed-space-level form of the same adjunction is
  [Stacks Project, Tag 0096, \emph{Sheaves on Spaces}, \S Pullback and pushforward of
  modules].}
  For a morphism of presheaves of rings \(\varphi\), the extension-of-scalars functor
  \(\mathtt{extendScalars}\,\varphi\) on presheaves of modules is left adjoint to
  restriction of scalars along \(\varphi\). When \(\varphi\) comes from a ring
  isomorphism, restriction of scalars is moreover strong monoidal
  (\(\mathtt{restrictScalarsMonoidalOfRingEquiv}\)).
nodes.title: Extension of scalars is left adjoint to restriction of scalars

nodes.statement:
\textit{Source: [Stacks Project], Sheaves on Spaces,
  Lemma~\texttt{lemma-adjoint-pullback-pushforward-modules} (Tag 0096).}
  For an adjunction \(F \dashv G\) of base sites together with compatible morphisms of the
  structure presheaves, the presheaf-of-modules pushforwards along \(F\) and \(G\) are
  adjoint; applied to the open-immersion adjunction this yields \(f^{*} \dashv f_{*}\) on
  module sheaves. The right-adjoint witnesses \(\mathtt{pushforward₀IsRightAdjoint}\),
  \(\mathtt{restrictScalarsIsRightAdjoint}\) and the assembled
  \(\mathtt{pullback0Adjunction}\) package the scheme-level form.
nodes.title: Pullback is left adjoint to pushforward on module sheaves

nodes.statement:
\textit{Source: [Stacks Project], Sheaves on Spaces, Lemma~\texttt{lemma-points-exactness}
  (Tag 007T), part (3).}
  If a morphism \(g\colon M \to N\) of presheaves of \(R\)-modules on
  \(\mathtt{Opens}\,X\) induces an isomorphism on the \(\mathrm{Ab}\)-stalk at \(x\),
  then the \(R_x\)-linear stalk map \cref{lem:stalk_linear_map} is bijective and hence
  upgrades to an \(R_x\)-linear equivalence \(M_x \xrightarrow{\sim} N_x\)
  (\(\mathtt{stalkLinearEquivOfIsIso}\)).
nodes.title: A module morphism is a stalkwise isomorphism iff its stalk maps are bijective
Local injectivity is equivalent to injectivity on stalks

nodes.statement:
\textit{Source: [Stacks Project], Modules on Ringed Spaces, \S Flat modules,
  Definition~\texttt{definition-flat}.}
  If \(F\) is a sectionwise-flat presheaf of \(R\)-modules and \(g\) lies in the
  sheafification localizer \(J.W\), then both whiskerings \(F \triangleleft g\) and
  \(g \triangleright F\) again lie in \(J.W\); in particular \(F \triangleleft g\) is
  \(J\)-locally injective. This is the flatness-dependent predecessor of the
  unconditional \cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whiskerleft_via_stalk}.
nodes.title: Tensoring with a flat module preserves the weak-equivalence class

nodes.statement:
\textit{Source: [Stacks Project], Sites and Sheaves,
  Lemma~\texttt{lemma-localize-special-cocontinuous} (Tag 03CH). The Stacks Project's
  vocabulary for this comparison is ``special cocontinuous functor'' rather than
  ``dense subsite''; the two describe the same phenomenon, and this is the closest
  verbatim match to the over-slice statement below.}
  For an open \(U\) of a scheme, the topological site \(\mathtt{Opens}\,U\) embeds as a
  dense subsite of the over-slice site, inducing an equivalence between the categories of
  \(A\)-valued sheaves on \(U\) and on the slice; the sieve-cover comparison
  \(\mathtt{overEquiv\_image\_cover\_iff}\) and the dense-subsite instance
  \(\mathtt{overEquivInverseIsDenseSubsite}\) are its ingredients.
nodes.title: Sheaf-category equivalence induced by an over-slice dense subsite

nodes.statement:
[Substrate \(\otimes\) respects a pair of isomorphisms]
  
  
  
  For \(M \cong M'\) and \(N \cong N'\) in \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\), sheafifying the
  presheaf-of-modules tensor of the underlying presheaf isomorphisms produces an
  isomorphism \(M \otimes_X N \cong M' \otimes_X N'\). This is the functor-of-isomorphisms
  form of the substrate tensor, used to build the middle-four interchange and the
  well-definedness of the multiplication on iso-classes.

[\(\mathtt{tensorObjIsoOfIso}\) is bifunctorial in composition]
  
  
  
  For composable isomorphisms \(M \xrightarrow{e_1} M' \xrightarrow{e_2} M''\) and
  \(N \xrightarrow{e_1'} N' \xrightarrow{e_2'} N''\) in \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\),
  \[
    \mathtt{tensorObjIsoOfIso}\,(e_1 \ggg e_2)\,(e_1' \ggg e_2')
      = \mathtt{tensorObjIsoOfIso}\,e_1\,e_1' \,\ggg\, \mathtt{tensorObjIsoOfIso}\,e_2\,e_2'.
  \]
  Tensor-transport of a composite of isomorphisms is the composite of the transports.

[\(\mathtt{tensorObjIsoOfIso}\) preserves identities]
  
  
  
  \(\mathtt{tensorObjIsoOfIso}\,(\mathrm{Iso.refl}\,M)\,(\mathrm{Iso.refl}\,N)
    = \mathrm{Iso.refl}\,(M \otimes_X N)\): the tensor-transport of a pair of identities is the
  identity.

[Threefold tensor/composition interchange]
  
  
  In a monoidal category \(C\), for morphisms \(a_i : X_i \to Y_i \to Z_i \to W_i\)
  (\(i = 1,2\)) presented as three composable legs on each tensor factor,
  \[
    (a_1^{(1)} \ggg a_1^{(2)} \ggg a_1^{(3)}) \otimes (a_2^{(1)} \ggg a_2^{(2)} \ggg a_2^{(3)})
      = (a_1^{(1)} \otimes a_2^{(1)}) \ggg (a_1^{(2)} \otimes a_2^{(2)}) \ggg (a_1^{(3)} \otimes a_2^{(3)}).
  \]
  The tensor of two threefold composites is the threefold composite of the tensors.

[Functor image of the threefold interchange]
  
  
  
  For a functor \(F : C \to D\) out of a monoidal category, the \(F\)-image of the
  threefold tensor/composition interchange (\cref{lem:tensorhom_comp3}) splits as the
  threefold composite of the \(F\)-images of the per-leg tensors.

[Presheaf dual-transport is contravariantly functorial in composition]
  
  
  
  For composable presheaf-of-modules isomorphisms \(L \xrightarrow{e_1} L' \xrightarrow{e_2} L''\),
  \[
    \mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,(e_1 \ggg e_2)
      = \mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,e_2 \,\ggg\, \mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,e_1,
  \]
  the order reversing because the dual is built by \emph{pre}composition (it is contravariant).

[Presheaf dual-transport preserves identities]
  
  
  
  \(\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,(\mathrm{Iso.refl}\,L) = \mathrm{Iso.refl}\,(L^{\vee})\) at the presheaf
  level.

[Sheaf-level dual-transport is contravariantly functorial in composition]
  
  
  
  For composable isomorphisms \(L \xrightarrow{e_1} L' \xrightarrow{e_2} L''\) in
  \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\),
  \[
    \mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,(e_1 \ggg e_2)
      = \mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,e_2 \,\ggg\, \mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,e_1.
  \]

[Sheaf-level dual-transport preserves identities]
  
  
  
  \(\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,(\mathrm{Iso.refl}\,L) = \mathrm{Iso.refl}\,(L^{\vee})\) at the sheaf
  level.

[\(\mathcal{O}_X \otimes_X \mathcal{O}_X \cong \mathcal{O}_X\)]
  
  
  
  The substrate tensor of the structure sheaf with itself is the structure sheaf:
  \(\mathtt{tensorObj}\,\mathcal{O}_X\,\mathcal{O}_X \cong \mathcal{O}_X\), where
  \(\mathcal{O}_X = \mathtt{SheafOfModules.unit}\,X.\mathtt{ringCatSheaf}\). It is the unit
  isomorphism consumed by the invertibility constructions.

[Middle-four interchange for \(\otimes_X\)]
  
  
  
  For arbitrary \(A,B,C,D \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) there is an isomorphism
  \((A \otimes B) \otimes (C \otimes D) \cong (A \otimes C) \otimes (B \otimes D)\),
  reassociating the four factors. It is the bookkeeping isomorphism used to show
  \(\otimes\)-invertibility is closed under tensor product.

[Unit comparison is an iso from a finality hypothesis]
  
  
  For \(g : Y \to X\) with \((\mathtt{Opens.map}\,g.\mathtt{base}).\mathtt{Final}\), the
  Mathlib unit comparison \(\mathtt{pullbackObjUnitToUnit}\,g\) is an isomorphism.

[Bundled iso form of the unit comparison]
  
  
  
  For \(g : Y \to X\) with \((\mathtt{Opens.map}\,g.\mathtt{base}).\mathtt{Final}\), the
  isomorphism \(g^*\mathcal{O}_X \cong \mathcal{O}_Y\) bundling the unit comparison
  \(\mathtt{pullbackObjUnitToUnit}\,g\) of \cref{lem:isiso_pbu_of_final}. Handing out the
  bundled \(\mathtt{Iso}\) keeps downstream reasoning at the \(\mathtt{Iso}\) level.

[Sheafification--monoidality reconciliation brick]
  
  
  
  For presheaves of modules \(P,Q\) on \(X\), sheafifying the presheaf tensor \(P \otimes Q\)
  agrees with sheafifying the tensor of the underlying presheaves of their sheafifications.
  This is the ``sheafification is monoidal'' reconciliation bridging a presheaf-level
  tensorator with the substrate \(\otimes_X\).

[\(\mathtt{hom}\) of the reconciliation brick as a whisker composite]
  
  
  
  The \(\mathtt{hom}\) of \cref{def:sheafify_tensor_unit_iso} equals the sheafification of
  \(\eta_P \rhd Q\) followed by the sheafification of \((aP).\mathrm{val} \lhd \eta_Q\),
  stated on the common \(\circ\,\mathtt{forget}_2\) carrier so the two whisker factors can
  be merged.

[\(\mathtt{hom}\) of the reconciliation brick as one \(\mathtt{tensorHom}\)]
  
  
  
  The \(\mathtt{hom}\) of \cref{def:sheafify_tensor_unit_iso} equals the sheafification of
  the single \(\mathtt{tensorHom}\) \(\eta_P \otimes \eta_Q\) of the two sheafification-unit
